function.name: log_classification
function.description: Invoke this tool BEFORE every spoken response, on every turn that is part of substantive interview content. DO NOT invoke during turns that are not interview content: (a) the pre-interview flow (identity verification, introduction, consent, screening, or the Session 2/3 identity-check turns); (b) the closing protocol (the open-ended close question, the transition line, the scheduling question, the read-back-and-confirm exchange, the schedule_next_session invocation itself, the Session 3 closing line, and any post-close turns). Those scripted procedural exchanges have no classifiable analytic content and logging them only adds latency. Begin invoking from Phase 1 Milestone 1.1 onward (Session 1) and from the equivalent first interview-content milestone in Sessions 2 and 3, and STOP invoking the moment you enter the closing protocol. The tool is silent — it produces NO audible output to the participant. Its purpose is to log the precedence-walk classification of the participant's last utterance for analyst review and quality auditing. After invoking the tool, immediately produce your spoken response (the next thing in the same turn). Never include classification text in your spoken response itself — that text goes ONLY in the tool arguments, never as speech. The tool runs asynchronously; you do not wait for a result.

precedence_walk.description: The full precedence-ladder walk in compact form. Show each category tested and whether it matched. Example: "tested CONTRADICTORY (no), DEFLECTIVE (no), FRAME_SHIFT (no), EMOTIONAL_SIGNAL (no), HIGH_VALUE_THEME (no), EMERGENT_SIGNAL (no), → SUFFICIENT — specific dish: irio". This visible walk is required (not optional) — testing showed the walk itself improves classification accuracy.

final_classification.description: The single final category your precedence walk resolved to.
